content.problemStatement:
A repetition-free number is one in which each digit {1,2,3,…,9}\{1,2,3,…,9\}{1,2,3,…,9} appears at most once and the digit 000 does not appear. A repetition-free number can have at most nine digits, but may also have fewer than nine digits. Some examples of repetition-free numbers are 9,32,489,987619, 32, 489, 987619,32,489,98761 and 983245983245983245.

You will be given an integer NNN with at most nine digits. Your task is to print out the smallest repetition-free number bigger than NNN. For example, for 999999 the answer is 123123123, for 881881881 the answer is 891891891, and for 133133133 the answer is 134134134.

Input:A single line with a single integer with at most 999 digits.

Output:A single line containing the smallest repetition-free number bigger than the given number. If there is no repetition-free number bigger than the given number, print 000.
constraints: N consists of digits from 0 to 9
content.inputFormat: A single line with a single integer with at most 999 digits.
content.outputFormat: A single line containing the smallest repetition-free number bigger than the given number. If there is no repetition-free number bigger than the given number, print 000.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The smallest repetition-free number bigger than 99 is 123.
The smallest repetition-free number bigger than 999 is 1234.
The smallest repetition-free number bigger than 1234 is 1235.

solution.approach: To find the smallest repetition-free number bigger than the given number, we can start by checking if the given number is already repetition-free. If it is, we can try to increment the number while keeping it repetition-free. If it's not, we can try to find the smallest repetition-free number that is bigger than the given number. We can use a backtracking approach to generate all possible repetition-free numbers and find the smallest one that is bigger than the given number.
solution.timeComplexity: O(10^length of N) — because in the worst case, we have to generate all possible repetition-free numbers of the same length as the given number.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(length of N) — because we need to store the current repetition-free number being generated.
